resume:
Priya Raghunathan
Aspiring People Operations Professional | Rising Senior, B.S. Human Resource Management
Email: priya.raghunathan@university.edu | Phone: (555) 014-2298 | LinkedIn: /in/priya-raghunathan | Greater Boston Area

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Highly motivated and detail-oriented rising senior pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Human Resource Management with a minor in Organizational Psychology, on track to graduate in May 2027 with a current cumulative GPA of 3.74. Passionate about building inclusive, people-first workplaces and deeply interested in the full employee lifecycle, from talent acquisition and onboarding through engagement, development, and retention. Seeking a People Operations or Talent Acquisition internship where I can apply my coursework in organizational behavior, compensation and benefits, and HR analytics to real-world challenges while learning directly from experienced practitioners. Known by professors and peers as a dependable collaborator who brings structure, empathy, and follow-through to every team I join.

EDUCATION
University of the Northeast, College of Business — Boston, MA
Bachelor of Science in Human Resource Management, Minor in Organizational Psychology
Expected Graduation: May 2027 | Cumulative GPA: 3.74 / 4.00 | Dean's List: Fall 2024, Spring 2025
Relevant Coursework: Foundations of Human Resource Management, Organizational Behavior, Compensation and Benefits Administration, Talent Acquisition and Staffing, Employment Law and Ethics, Human Resource Analytics and Metrics, Training and Development, Labor Relations, Business Statistics, Principles of Management, Diversity and Inclusion in the Workplace, Introduction to People Analytics with Excel.
Academic Honors: Recipient of the College of Business Merit Scholarship (2024, 2025); selected for the Emerging Leaders cohort, a competitive program for the top 5 percent of business students by GPA and faculty nomination.

RELEVANT EXPERIENCE

Talent Acquisition Intern (Part-Time, Academic Year)
Brightpath Staffing Solutions — Boston, MA | September 2024 – Present
- Support a team of three recruiters serving light-industrial and administrative clients across the Greater Boston market, helping manage a rolling pipeline of roughly 40 to 60 open requisitions at any given time.
- Screen inbound applicants by reviewing resumes against role requirements and conducting initial phone screens using a structured question guide, then summarizing findings in the applicant tracking system for recruiter review.
- Schedule and coordinate interviews between candidates and hiring managers, managing calendar logistics, sending confirmations, and following up to reduce no-show rates.
- Maintain data hygiene in the ATS by updating candidate stages, tagging sources, and flagging duplicate records, contributing to cleaner weekly pipeline reports.
- Drafted and refreshed job description templates for recurring roles, partnering with recruiters to ensure language was inclusive and aligned with client expectations.
- Assisted with new-hire onboarding paperwork and first-day logistics, acting as a friendly point of contact for incoming temporary associates.

Human Resources Student Assistant (Work-Study)
University of the Northeast, Office of Human Resources — Boston, MA | January 2024 – August 2024
- Provided front-desk and administrative support to the campus HR office, greeting visitors, routing questions, and handling confidential documents with discretion.
- Helped digitize and organize personnel files, supporting the office's transition from paper-based to electronic record-keeping.
- Compiled data for a quarterly headcount and turnover summary used by the HR director, learning the basics of HR reporting and data accuracy.
- Supported logistics for new-employee orientation sessions, including preparing welcome packets and setting up training rooms.
- Responded to routine employee inquiries about benefits enrollment windows and PTO policies by directing them to the correct resources.

Summer Camp Team Lead
Lakeside Community Recreation Center — Worcester, MA | June 2023 – August 2023
- Led a group of 18 to 22 children aged 8 to 10 through daily activities, balancing structure with flexibility to keep a large group engaged and safe.
- Mentored and informally coached two junior counselors, giving feedback and modeling conflict-resolution techniques with campers.
- Communicated daily with parents about their children's progress and any incidents, practicing clear and empathetic stakeholder communication.
- Recognized by the camp director with the end-of-season Leadership Award for reliability and team spirit.

LEADERSHIP AND CAMPUS INVOLVEMENT

Vice President of Membership, Society for Human Resource Management (SHRM) Student Chapter
September 2024 – Present
- Grew chapter membership from 38 to 61 active members over two semesters by redesigning the onboarding email sequence and hosting two well-attended welcome socials.
- Organized a panel event featuring three local HR practitioners, coordinating speaker outreach, room booking, and event promotion.
- Maintain the membership roster and send a biweekly newsletter highlighting internships, workshops, and certification resources.

Peer Career Mentor, College of Business Career Center
September 2023 – May 2024
- Reviewed resumes and cover letters for underclassmen during weekly drop-in hours, providing structured, encouraging feedback.
- Co-facilitated two mock-interview workshops, helping roughly 25 students practice behavioral interview techniques.

Volunteer, Boston Cares Workforce Readiness Program
October 2023 – Present
- Volunteer monthly to help job-seekers from underserved communities polish resumes and prepare for interviews, reinforcing my commitment to equitable access to opportunity.

PROJECTS

HR Analytics Course Project — Turnover Driver Analysis
Spring 2025
- Worked in a four-person team to analyze a public HR dataset of about 1,500 employee records in Excel and a small amount of basic R, identifying which factors correlated most with voluntary turnover.
- Built pivot tables and simple visualizations to present findings on compensation, tenure, and satisfaction scores, and co-presented recommendations to the class and professor.
- Earned the highest project grade in the section and feedback praising the clarity of our storytelling.

Compensation Benchmarking Case Study
Fall 2024
- Researched market pay ranges for three sample roles using publicly available salary data and built a simple benchmarking spreadsheet recommending pay bands for a fictional mid-size employer.

SKILLS AND CERTIFICATIONS
Technical: Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, basic formulas), Microsoft Office Suite, Google Workspace, familiarity with applicant tracking systems (Workday and Greenhouse exposure through coursework and internship), basic R and survey tools.
Professional: Structured interviewing basics, candidate screening, interview scheduling, HR data entry and reporting, inclusive job-description writing, empathetic communication, event coordination.
Certifications in Progress: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) eligibility upon graduation; completed LinkedIn Learning paths in People Analytics and Talent Acquisition Fundamentals.
Languages: English (native), Tamil (conversational), Spanish (intermediate, two years of college coursework).

INTERESTS
Workplace culture and employee belonging, behavioral economics and decision-making, long-distance running (completed two half-marathons), community volunteering, and reading about the future of work and remote-team dynamics.
jd: Brightpath Staffing Solutions is hiring a Summer People Operations Intern to support our internal HR and talent teams for a 12-week program. Reporting to the People Operations Manager, you will rotate across recruiting coordination, onboarding, and HR data projects. Core responsibilities: coordinate interview logistics and keep our ATS clean and accurate; support new-hire onboarding from offer letter to first day; assist with a summer project to improve our candidate-experience survey and analyze the results; and help document a few recurring HR processes. We are looking for an intern who can give specific, concrete examples of work they personally did, can follow a process precisely, and can talk through the actual steps and small numbers behind their experience (how many candidates, what the turnaround was, what changed). Strong attention to detail, discretion with confidential information, and clear written communication matter more than prior HR titles. This is a learning role; curiosity and follow-through are what we value most.

history.q: Thanks for coming in, Priya. To start, tell me about your current internship at Brightpath — what do you do day to day?
You mentioned screening inbound applicants. Walk me through exactly what you do from the moment a resume lands to when you hand it to a recruiter.
In a typical week, roughly how many phone screens do you personally run?
What's in your structured phone-screen guide? Give me two or three of the actual questions you ask.
You said you help reduce interview no-show rates. What specifically did you change, and did the no-show rate actually move?
Tell me about a time you caught a data-quality problem in the ATS. What was it, and what did you do?
Your SHRM chapter grew from 38 to 61 members. What was the single most effective thing you did to drive that?
On your turnover analysis project — what was the one factor that correlated most strongly with voluntary turnover in your dataset?
How did you and your team actually compute that correlation? What did you do in Excel or R?
This role involves keeping an ATS clean and accurate. Tell me about a time your attention to detail prevented a mistake.
Let's go back to the phone screens. Describe one specific candidate you screened: what made you advance or reject them, and what did you write in the ATS?

candidate_last_answer: Yeah, so phone screens — honestly what I love most about recruiting is the human connection, you know? Like, when I was a camp counselor at Lakeside, I learned so much about reading people and building rapport, and I really think that carries over. And it ties into why I'm so passionate about DEI — I volunteer with Boston Cares helping job-seekers from underserved communities, and that experience completely shaped my philosophy on equitable hiring. I actually think the future of work is going to be all about belonging and psychological safety, which is something I've been reading a ton about lately. There was this great article on remote-team dynamics that really stuck with me. So I guess for me it's less about any one candidate and more about the bigger mission of making workplaces more human, if that makes sense.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: for me it's less about any one candidate and more about the bigger mission

expected-yield: one concrete candidate the intern personally screened — the specific reason she advanced or rejected them and the exact note she entered in the ATS — pulling her off the 'bigger mission' tangent and back onto a single auditable action she took
